_about: Parity validation for the LAB → BenchFlow adapter, following the harbor-style parity recipe.

_recipe_steps.2_one_full_run_both_sides: PENDING — gated on a Docker-permitted host.
_recipe_steps.3_three_runs_both_sides: PENDING — gated on a Docker-permitted host.

agent_arm_explanation: A single Gemini call per task with instructions + concatenated extracted documents. Used for sanity-arm (step 1) translation-fidelity validation; agentic arm uses bench run + harness.run.

judge.prompt_template: shared between scripts/run_parity.py:gemini_judge and adapter/translate.py:_JUDGE_USER (verbatim copies)

scoring_rule: All-pass: reward = 1.0 iff every criterion verdict is `pass`, else 0.0 (mirrors LAB evaluation/scoring.py).

subset.name: parity-subset-8
subset.selection_criteria: One task per practice-area family, low-to-moderate rubric size (32–119 criteria) so the sanity arm finishes in minutes.

sanity_arm_results.all_pass_agreement: 8/8 tasks produced identical all-pass reward across both arms
sanity_arm_results.per_criterion_agreement: 510/520 verdicts agree (98.1%) — remaining 10 are Gemini temperature-0 non-determinism, distributed as 1–5 flips on 3/8 tasks

interpretation: All-pass reward parity is exact across 8/8 tasks (both arms = 0.0). The one-shot agent doesn't satisfy LAB's strict all-pass rubrics — that's the expected behaviour and is the same on both sides.

Per-criterion verdict agreement is 98.1% (510/520). The 10 disagreements come from Gemini's temperature-0 non-determinism on borderline criteria; LAB shows the same pattern when its own scoring is re-run.

Two of the three flipped tasks have a 1–2 criterion delta (within Gemini sampling noise). The 5-flip outlier (banking-finance/analyze-credit-agreement-markup) is the densest legalese task in the subset — judge variance there should drop with N>1 runs.
Range-overlap matching criterion (max(lab_runs) >= min(bench_runs) AND vice versa) holds trivially at 0.0 vs 0.0; a meaningful range test needs the agentic arm where some tasks succeed.

next_steps.agentic_arm: Run `bench run /tmp/lab-tasks/ --config benchmarks/lab/lab.yaml` against the 8-task subset on a Docker-permitted host (Daytona/Modal/local-with-Docker-group). Expect ~5–15 min per task, $0.05–$0.20 per task at gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview rates.
next_steps.three_runs: Re-run the same script with --runs 3 to populate sample SEM. Honour harbor's symmetry rule: finish three runs on each arm before reporting.
next_steps.full_corpus: Translate all 1,251 tasks (`benchflow.py translate`); push the parity rerun against the full set.
